instructions.instruction: Save all artifacts (screenshots, extracted JSON, theme docs) under .context/artifacts/<website-slug>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/
instructions.original_snippets:
All artifacts (screenshots, extracted JSON, theme docs) **must** be saved under:
.context/artifacts/<website-slug>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/
...SLUG="designandbloom-co-uk"   # replace with actual slug
DATE=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
ARTIFACTS=".context/artifacts/${SLUG}/${DATE}"
instructions.relevant_when: Any time the agent saves screenshots, JSON dumps, or theme documentation during a theme porting task

instructions.instruction: NEVER use /tmp for artifact storage — always use .context/artifacts/<website-slug>/<YYYY-MM-DD>/
instructions.original_snippets:
**NEVER use `/tmp` for any artifact storage.**
/tmp is ephemeral, session-scoped, and not tracked — artifacts saved there are lost.
instructions.relevant_when: When the agent chooses where to save screenshots or extracted data

instructions.instruction: Use the capture method decision tree: prefer Method A (agent-browser) if available, then Method B (curl/source inspection), then Method C (manual DevTools)
instructions.original_snippets:
Can you run agent-browser in this environment?
  YES → use Method A (automated browser extraction)
  NO ↓
Is the site's HTML/CSS source accessible?
  YES → use Method B (source inspection)
  NO ↓
Can you manually inspect the live site in browser DevTools?
  YES → use Method C (manual inspection)
instructions.relevant_when: At the start of Stage 1 when choosing how to extract design tokens

instructions.instruction: For SPAs add a scroll-and-wait step before extracting: window.scrollTo + wait networkidle + wait 2000ms
instructions.original_snippets:
**If the target is a SPA (React, Next.js, Vue, etc.):**
agent-browser eval 'window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)'
agent-browser wait --load networkidle
agent-browser wait --timeout 2000
instructions.relevant_when: When the target site is a single-page application (React, Next.js, Vue)

instructions.instruction: If CSS custom properties are empty (no :root vars), switch to computed-only element extraction and note 'source uses hardcoded values' in overview.md
instructions.original_snippets:
**If the `colors` object is empty (site uses no CSS custom properties):**
The site styles everything with hardcoded values. Switch to computed-only extraction...Note in `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/overview.md`: "source uses hardcoded values — no design token system detected"
instructions.relevant_when: When extracting tokens and the :root CSS custom properties object is empty

instructions.instruction: Write all theme documentation to ${ARTIFACTS}/theme/ before touching any code — required files: colours.md, typography.md, spacing.md, components.md, overview.md
instructions.original_snippets:
Write the findings into `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/` before touching any code.
| `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/colours.md` | All extracted colours...
| `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/typography.md` | Font families...
| `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/spacing.md` | Padding/margin...
| `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/components.md` | Button, card...
| `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/overview.md` | Summary, source URL...
instructions.relevant_when: At the start of Stage 2 (Document) before writing any CSS code

instructions.instruction: Document colours with hex, HSL, and semantic role in a table in colours.md
instructions.original_snippets:
| Token | Hex | HSL | Role |
|-------|-----|-----|------|
| --primary | #0D9488 | 174 90% 30% | CTA buttons, links |
instructions.relevant_when: When writing the colours.md documentation file during Stage 2

instructions.instruction: NEVER wrap HSL values in hsl() inside @theme inline — use bare channel values (e.g. 174 90% 31%) in @theme inline and hsl() only in :root
instructions.relevant_when: When writing @theme inline in src/index.css during Stage 3

instructions.instruction: Map all semantic colour tokens via CSS variables in :root and reference them through @theme inline — never use arbitrary Tailwind values like bg-[#abc]
instructions.original_snippets:
**NEVER use arbitrary Tailwind values (`bg-[#abc]`) for semantic colours.**
Always define a CSS variable token in `:root` and map it via `@theme inline`.
// ✅ GOOD
<button className="bg-primary text-primary-foreground px-4 py-2 rounded-[var(--radius)]">
instructions.relevant_when: When applying theme to React components during Stage 3

instructions.instruction: Define a --radius CSS variable in :root for the project-wide border radius token
instructions.relevant_when: When writing src/index.css during Stage 3

instructions.instruction: Add font to index.html via <link rel='preconnect'> and Google Fonts link with display=swap and all needed weights
instructions.original_snippets:
### 3b. Add font to `index.html`
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />
instructions.relevant_when: When a Google Font is detected during extraction and needs to be applied to the project

instructions.instruction: Update components page-by-page starting with shared layout components (Header, Footer) then individual pages
instructions.original_snippets: Work page-by-page, starting with shared layout components (Header, Footer) then pages.
instructions.relevant_when: During Stage 3 when applying extracted theme to React components

instructions.instruction: NEVER extract colours from :hover pseudo-states — always extract from the resting state
instructions.original_snippets:
**NEVER extract colours from `:hover` pseudo-states.**
`getComputedStyle` on a hovered element requires the pointer to be physically over it. The value may silently return the base state instead, giving a misleading token.
instructions.relevant_when: When extracting colour tokens from interactive elements like buttons and links

instructions.instruction: NEVER use prefers-color-scheme dark mode colours as base tokens — always extract in light mode
instructions.original_snippets:
**NEVER use `prefers-color-scheme` media query colours as your base tokens.**
Always extract in light mode, or explicitly document which mode was active.
instructions.relevant_when: When running extraction on a machine that may be in dark mode

instructions.instruction: NEVER copy the source site's CSS files into the project — rebuild every value from scratch
instructions.original_snippets:
**NEVER copy the source site's actual CSS files or stylesheets.**
Import conflicts, class name collisions, and specificity wars will corrupt your Tailwind output.
instructions.relevant_when: When applying the extracted theme — choosing whether to copy or rebuild CSS

instructions.instruction: Load references/extraction.md at start of Stage 1, references/tailwind-mapping.md at start of Stage 2, references/verification.md at start of Stage 4
instructions.original_snippets:
> **Load before starting this stage:**
> Read `references/extraction.md` in full...
> **Do NOT load** `references/tailwind-mapping.md` or `references/verification.md` yet.
instructions.relevant_when: At the transition between each stage of the workflow

instructions.instruction: Note the extraction method used (agent-browser / curl / manual DevTools) in ${ARTIFACTS}/theme/overview.md
instructions.original_snippets:
Note in `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/overview.md`: "extraction method: source inspection (curl)".
...Note in `${ARTIFACTS}/theme/overview.md`: "extraction method: manual DevTools inspection".
instructions.relevant_when: At the end of Stage 1 when saving documentation

instructions.instruction: Use the Token Decision Tree from references/tailwind-mapping.md to assign ambiguous colours to semantic tokens
instructions.original_snippets:
Use the **Token Decision Tree** in `references/tailwind-mapping.md` to assign ambiguous colours.
...Is it the most prominent interactive colour? YES → --primary...
instructions.relevant_when: During Stage 2 when a colour does not obviously map to a single semantic token

instructions.instruction: Define @layer base styles in src/index.css: body background-color, color, font-family using CSS variables
instructions.relevant_when: When writing src/index.css during Stage 3

instructions.instruction: Run visual diff against the original site — aim for <10% mismatch on primary pages before considering the port complete
instructions.original_snippets: Aim for < 10% mismatch on primary pages (home, about) before considering the port complete.
instructions.relevant_when: During Stage 4 verification after applying the theme

instructions.instruction: Use the iteration loop: run diff → identify token/component causing mismatch → update src/index.css → rebuild → re-run diff
instructions.original_snippets:
1. Run diff → note red areas
2. Identify which token/component causes the mismatch
3. Update `src/index.css` or the affected component
4. Rebuild: `bun run build && bun run preview`
5. Re-run diff
6. Repeat until mismatch % is acceptable
instructions.relevant_when: During Stage 4 when the visual diff shows mismatches that need fixing
